intro.en: The land of pandas, fiery hotpot, and a pace of life that redefines 'relaxed'. Sip tea in a bamboo grove while locals play mahjong — this is Chengdu.
intro.zh: 熊猫之乡，火锅之城，一座重新定义'安逸'的城市。竹林里喝茶，听隔壁桌搓麻将——这就是成都。

desc.en: See pandas at their most active — early morning feeding time is pure joy. Watch cubs tumble and adults munch bamboo with unmatched dedication.
desc.zh: 看熊猫最活跃的时刻——清晨喂食时间萌到犯规。幼崽打滚，成年熊猫专注啃竹子。

highlight.zh: 熊猫幼崽育婴室（季节性）

tips.en: Arrive at 7:30 AM sharp — pandas sleep by 10 AM. The moon gate entrance is less crowded.
tips.zh: 7:30准时到——10点后熊猫就睡了。月亮门入口人少些。

location.en: Chenghua District, 18km north of city center
location.zh: 成华区，市中心以北18公里

tags.zh: 野生动物 · 亲子 · 清晨

desc.en: Red lanterns, wooden architecture, and the best street food within the Wuhou Shrine complex. Perfect at dusk when lanterns flicker on.
desc.zh: 红灯笼、木建筑、武侯祠旁最棒的街头小吃街。黄昏时分灯笼亮起，氛围满分。

hours.en: Open all day, best after 5 PM

tips.en: Try 'san da pao' (three big cannons) — a traditional snack with a theatrical preparation.
tips.zh: 尝尝'三大炮'——制作过程有表演效果的传统小吃。

location.en: Wuhou District, adjacent to Wuhou Shrine

tags.zh: 街头美食 · 夜景 · 文化

desc.en: Birthplace of Taoism — misty peaks, ancient temples hidden in dense forest. The front mountain is easier; the back mountain is wilder and far less crowded.
desc.zh: 道教发源地——雾绕峰峦，密林藏古观。前山较平缓，后山更野，游客少得多。

tips.en: Take the high-speed train to Qingchengshan station (30min from Chengdu).
tips.zh: 成都坐高铁到青城山站仅30分钟。

tags.zh: 世界遗产 · 自然 · 一日游

desc.en: A cauldron of bubbling chili oil, Sichuan peppercorns, and infinite ingredients. The numbing-spicy (mala) sensation is unlike anything else.
desc.zh: 翻滚的红油锅底，花椒与干辣椒共舞，食材无限。麻辣鲜香四重奏，没有体验过的人生不完整。

where.en: Hotpot restaurants are everywhere — follow the aroma and the crowds
where.zh: 火锅店遍布全城——循着香气和人潮走就对了

must_try.en: Order 'huanghou' (beef aorta) and lotus root slices — trust us.
must_try.zh: 黄喉和藕片必点——信我们。

desc.en: A humble bowl of noodles with minced pork, preserved vegetables, and chili oil. Simple, perfect, and under ¥15.
desc.zh: 肉末、芽菜、红油浇在手擀面上。简单、完美、不过15块钱。

where.en: Any local noodle shop — look for long queues
where.zh: 街边面馆——看到有人排队就对了

must_try.en: Add a spoon of the house chili paste on the side.
must_try.zh: 旁边辣椒酱舀一勺进去。

desc.en: Tea houses are Chengdu's living room. Bamboo chairs, jasmine tea, ear cleaning services — spend an entire afternoon doing nothing. It's an art form.
desc.zh: 茶馆是成都人的客厅。竹椅、盖碗茶、掏耳朵——花一整个下午无所事事。这是一种艺术。

significance.en: Chengdu has over 10,000 tea houses — more than any city on Earth.
significance.zh: 成都茶馆超万家——全球之最。

experience.en: Renmin Park Heming Tea House — the most authentic
experience.zh: 人民公园鹤鸣茶社——最正宗的老茶馆

climate.en: Spring (Mar–May) & Autumn (Sep–Nov). Summer is hot and humid; winter is grey but mild.
climate.zh: 春秋最佳（3-5月、9-11月）。夏季湿热，冬季阴冷但温和。

transit.en: Metro is clean and easy (¥2–8). Bike sharing (Meituan/Hellobike) is the best way to explore.
transit.zh: 地铁干净便利（¥2–8）。共享单车（美团/哈啰）是最佳探索方式。

intercity.en: Chengdu Shuangliu (CTU) & Tianfu (TFU) airports. High-speed rail: 1h to Chongqing, 3h to Xi'an.
intercity.zh: 双流(CTU)+天府(TFU)双机场。高铁：1小时到重庆，3小时到西安。

tips.en: Sichuan food is SPICY — say 'bù là' (not spicy) if needed. Most restaurants can adjust. Bring tissues — many local spots don't provide napkins. Carry cash for street vendors.
tips.zh: 川菜辣——需要的话说'不要辣'。多数餐馆可以调整。随身带纸巾——很多小店不提供。带现金以备路边摊。

practicalGuide.en:
Getting around: Chengdu metro is excellent and cheap (¥2–8). Buy a 天府通 (Tianfu Tong) card at any station. Bike-sharing (Meituan/Hellobike) is everywhere — the best way to explore the city's flat terrain. Didi (Chinese Uber) costs ¥15–30 for most city rides.

Panda Base logistics: The research base opens at 7:30 AM. Take metro Line 3 to Panda Avenue station, then a free shuttle bus. Arrive at 7:30 sharp — pandas are most active in the morning and sleep by 10 AM. The moon-gate entrance is less crowded. Buy tickets online in advance (¥55).

Heat management: Chengdu summers are brutally humid (30°C+). Carry a portable fan and water. Most restaurants are not air-conditioned. Learn "微辣" (wēi là, mild spicy) — even "mild" in Sichuan is hot.

Language: English is not widely spoken outside the tourist areas. Download Pleco (offline Chinese dictionary). Learn "多少钱" (how much) and "买单" (check, please).
practicalGuide.zh:
出行：成都地铁方便便宜（¥2–8）。任何地铁站都可以买天府通卡。共享单车（美团/哈啰）满街都是——平坦的成都骑自行车最舒服。滴滴市内大部分¥15–30。

看熊猫攻略：繁育基地7:30开门。地铁3号线到熊猫大道站，转免费摆渡车。7:30准时到——熊猫早上最活跃，10点就睡了。月亮门入口人少。提前网上买票（¥55）。

防热：成都夏天湿热得厉害（30°C+）。随身带小风扇和水。大部分餐馆没空调。学会说"微辣"——在四川，微辣已经很辣了。

语言：景区以外英语不普及。下载Pleco（离线中文字典）。记住"多少钱"和"买单"这两句就够了。

honestTips.en:
Hotpot is hotter than you think. Even the "mild" broth in Chengdu will make you sweat. If you're not a spice veteran, request "鸳鸯锅" (yuanyang guo, half spicy half clear broth). The oil-based broth is not soup — don't drink it. The sesame oil dip isn't for flavor; it coats your stomach against the spice. Your clothes will smell for 2 days after.

Jinli vs Kuanzhai Xiangzi. Both are tourist streets with inflated prices. Jinli is more atmospheric at night. Kuanzhai is cleaner and more Instagram-friendly. Neither is where locals hang out — they're for tourists. Go once, take photos, don't spend much.

Panda Base crowds. Despite arriving at 7:30, expect queues for the panda nursery. On weekends, wait times can exceed 1 hour. Weekdays are significantly better. Skip the panda volunteer program — it's expensive (¥3,000+) and you barely touch a panda.
honestTips.zh:
火锅比你想象的辣得多。在成都，连"微辣"都能让你出汗。吃不了辣就点"鸳鸯锅"——一半红油一半清汤。那层红油不是汤——别喝。香油蘸碟不是调味的，是给胃上保护膜的。吃完衣服带味两天。

锦里vs宽窄巷子。两条都是游客街，价格虚高。锦里晚上更有氛围。宽窄更干净更适合拍照。但本地人都不去——去一次拍拍照就行了，别花大钱。

熊猫基地排队。就算7:30到，看熊猫幼崽还是要排队。周末等一小时以上。工作日好得多。别参加熊猫志愿者项目——¥3000+，其实基本碰不到熊猫。

localSecrets.en:
People's Park (Renmin Gongyuan). This is where Chengdu's soul lives. Find the Heming Tea House (鹤鸣茶社) — established 1923. Grab a bamboo chair, order jasmine tea (¥15–30), and watch locals play mahjong, get their ears cleaned, and argue about politics. Spend at least 2 hours doing nothing. It's called "安逸" (ānyì) — the art of comfortable idleness.

Late-night hotpot in Yulin. The Yulin neighborhood (where Zhao Lei's song "Chengdu" is set) comes alive after 10 PM with hotpot restaurants that stay open until 4 AM. Go with friends, order beer, and watch Chengdu's nightlife unfold.

Qingcheng Mountain back trail. Skip the front mountain (¥90, tourist trail with cable cars). Take the back mountain entrance (¥20, wild trail with waterfalls). It's more physically demanding but you'll have ancient Taoist temples almost to yourself. Take the high-speed train from Chengdu Xi'pu station (30 min, ¥16).
localSecrets.zh:
人民公园。成都的灵魂在这里。找到鹤鸣茶社（1923年开张），搬把竹椅，点杯盖碗茶（¥15–30），看本地人打麻将、掏耳朵、谈天说地。至少花两小时啥也不干。这叫"安逸"。

玉林夜火锅。赵雷唱"成都"的玉林路，晚上10点后火锅店开始热闹，营业到凌晨4点。叫上朋友，点几瓶啤酒，看成都的夜生活怎么过。

青城山后山。别去前山（¥90，游客路线有缆车）。走后山入口（¥20，野路有瀑布）。更费体力但你能独享古老的道观。成都犀浦站坐高铁30分钟到青城山站（¥16）。
